text: The manuscript cited Vol. 47, No. 3, pp. 1234–89 of the J. Phys. Chem., noting that samples required ≥99.9% purity and storage at −80°C±2°C in 50μg/mL aliquots.

reference_asr_transcript: The manuscript cited volume 47, number 3, pages 1234 to 89 of the Journal of Physical Chemistry, noting that samples required greater than or equal to 99.9% purity in storage at minus 80 degrees Celsius plus or minus 2 degrees Celsius and 50 micrograms per milliliter aliquots.

text: According to IEEE Trans. Vol. 15, pp. 67–142, the circuit required 3.3V±5%, 150mA max., with capacitance ≤10μF and operating temps between −40°C and +85°C per MIL-STD-883.
spoken_form: According to I triple E Transactions Volume fifteen, pages sixty-seven to one forty-two, the circuit required three point three volts plus or minus five percent, one hundred fifty milliamps max, with capacitance less than or equal to ten microfarads and operating temps between minus forty degrees Celsius and plus eighty-five degrees Celsius per MIL standard eight eighty-three.
reference_asr_transcript: According to IEEE Inzactions Volume 15, pages 67 to 142, the circuit required 3.3 volts plus or minus 5%, 150 milliamps max with capacitance less than or equal to 10 microfarads and operating temps between minus 40 degrees Celsius and plus 85 degrees Celsius per mil standard 883.

text: Administer 0.25mL of 1×10⁻⁴ mol/L (2R,3S,4R,5R)-2,3,4,5,6-pentahydroxyhexanal (CAS 50-99-7) to Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain S288C and Candida albicans strain SC5314 at 30.0°C ± 0.2°C maintaining pH 5.5 ± 0.05 per ASTM E2149-13a.
